reasoning: After comprehensive analysis, the probability that Ramzan Kadyrov will cease to be head of the Chechen Republic before June 1, 2025 is 8%. Key factors: (1) Historical pattern of public statements about stepping down followed by immediate retractions (2016, 2017, 2022); (2) May 6-7 pattern exactly repeats this - Kadyrov asked to be "relieved of duties" then clarified/walked it back same day; (3) No official Kremlin/Putin acceptance by May 8; (4) Extremely tight 3-week timeline for formal transition; (5) Putin's strategic interest in stability; (6) Health rumors persist but unconfirmed and Kadyrov continues public appearances; (7) Succession planning discussions about son Adam appear speculative; (8) Most recent source (Hudson Institute, May 9) still describes him as "the leader of Chechnya" with no indication of actual departure. The weight of evidence suggests this is political signaling rather than genuine intent for imminent transition.
